summary: This is a brief announcement post linking to O'Neil's Bloomberg column about the standoff between colleges and students/parents over tuition during COVID-19. The core argument is that colleges charging full tuition for online education creates a game of chicken scenario. O'Neil suggests that online education should be priced accordingly, and that the pandemic will force colleges to justify their value proposition.
key_points: COVID-19 has created a confrontation between colleges and families over the value of education
Online education should come at a reduced 'online price' rather than full tuition
The pandemic will force colleges to prove their worth to students and parents
There's an implicit standoff where neither side wants to blink first on pricing
money_quotes: Covid-19 Will Make Colleges Prove Their Worth
Online education should come at an online price
key_insight: The pandemic has turned college tuition into a high-stakes negotiation where institutions must finally justify whether their premium pricing delivers premium value.
